settings.label: What You Get
settings.body:
1 tablespoon (~10g) of live, active milk kefir grains in a sealed pouch. With basic care these multiply forever — by week 4 you'll have enough to share with friends. One spoon ferments 1L of milk every 24 hours.

  • 30+ identified strains of beneficial bacteria + yeasts
  • Lab-tested — same culture used by traditional fermenters in the Caucasus for centuries
  • Wakes up in your first batch — full strength by batch 3

settings.label: How To Start
settings.body:
1. Wake up — Add grains to 250ml whole milk, cover loosely with cloth. Sit 24h at room temperature.
  2. Strain — Pour through a plastic sieve (no metal). The kefir is the liquid; grains stay in the sieve.
  3. Repeat — Put grains in fresh 1L of milk, ferment another 24h. Drink the strained kefir.
  4. Build — By batch 3 the culture is at full strength. Grains will multiply over the first month.

settings.label: Care Basics
settings.body:
• Use whole, full-fat dairy milk — UHT works, fresh is better
  • Plastic, glass, or wood — never metal utensils or containers
  • Filtered water only when rinsing
  • Take a break — store grains in milk in fridge for up to 14 days between batches
  • Long pause — freeze in milk for up to 6 months

settings.label: STRAIN COUNT
settings.heading: 30+ strains identified
settings.description: Lactobacillus kefiri, L. acidophilus, L. lactis, kefir yeast strains and more — independently profiled by our lab.

settings.label: LAB-GRADE
settings.heading: Cleared for human food use
settings.description: Tested for absence of harmful contaminants every batch. No mold spores, no pathogenic E. coli, no salmonella.

settings.label: REUSABLE
settings.heading: Multiplies forever
settings.description: Treated right, your grains will outlive you. They double in mass every 2–4 weeks. Share, sell, or scale up your batches.

settings.label: CONSISTENT
settings.heading: Same flavor, batch after batch
settings.description: Standardized starter culture means your batch 30 tastes like your batch 3 — unlike wild ferments which drift.

settings.heading: WHY OUR CULTURE
settings.intro: Wild-harvested kefir grains are unpredictable. A lab-tested starter gives you the consistency professional fermenters rely on.

settings.heading: FROM POUCH TO
settings.tips_bullets:
Use plastic, glass, or wood — never metal
Full-fat whole milk works best (UHT is fine)
Kitchen-counter warm (24–28°C) is ideal
First batch may taste mild — full strength by batch 3
Grains keep growing — share or freeze the surplus

settings.heading: Wake the grains
settings.description: Tip the pouch into 250ml whole milk in a glass jar. Cover with breathable cloth. Sit 24 hours at room temp.

settings.heading: Strain & store
settings.description: Pour through a plastic sieve. The drinkable kefir is the liquid. Keep the grains for the next batch.

settings.heading: First real batch
settings.description: Drop grains into 1L fresh whole milk. Ferment 18–24 hours. Strain, drink, repeat — the grains do the work.

settings.heading: FIRST
settings.intro: It's a living thing — first batches feel weird. Here's what's normal so you don't panic.

settings.label: TASTE
settings.heading: Tangy, slightly fizzy
settings.description: Like a sour buttermilk with light effervescence. Stronger by batch 3 as the culture wakes up. If overly sour, ferment shorter.

settings.label: TEXTURE
settings.heading: Lumpy is normal
settings.description: Grains look like cottage cheese curds. The strained liquid may be slightly thicker than milk and have small clumps — totally fine.

settings.label: TIMING
settings.heading: 18–24 hours, no shortcuts
settings.description: Cooler kitchens (under 22°C) need 24–28h. Warmer ones (28°C+) need 16–20h. Trust the culture, not the clock.

settings.label: PROBIOTIC
settings.heading: 10⁹+ CFU per glass
settings.description: Live, viable cultures by the billion in every 250ml glass — far higher than any pasteurised store-bought kefir.

settings.label: AFFORDABLE
settings.heading: ₹40/L after first batch
settings.description: After your one-time grain cost, ongoing kefir cost is just the milk. Most home fermenters pay back the grains within 2 months.

settings.label: EDUCATION
settings.heading: Learn fermentation craft
settings.description: Kefir is the gateway to lactic-acid fermentation. Learn to read your culture, control flavour, and build intuition.

settings.heading: IS THIS
settings.yes_bullets:
Want consistent, high-strain kefir at home
Love DIY and reading what your culture is doing
Are comfortable with a 5-min daily routine
Want to lower your probiotic supplement spend
Enjoy gifting / sharing extra grains as they multiply
settings.no_bullets:
Can't drink dairy (try our water-kefir grains instead)
Want grab-and-go probiotics with no daily commitment
Travel for 4+ weeks without a kefir-sitter
Aren't comfortable handling living cultures
Want a sweet, smooth drink (kefir is tangy and slightly fizzy)

settings.label: PAUSE
settings.heading: Fridge break: 14 days
settings.description: Need to skip a few days? Cover grains in fresh milk and refrigerate. They go dormant. Resume normal batches when ready.

settings.label: NO METAL
settings.heading: Plastic, glass, wood only
settings.description: Metal utensils and containers damage the grains over time. Always use plastic strainers and glass jars.

settings.label: WATER
settings.heading: Filtered, never chlorinated
settings.description: If you ever need to rinse the grains (rare), use filtered water. Tap water chlorine kills the culture.

settings.label: BREATHE
settings.heading: Cloth cover, not airtight
settings.description: Kefir grains need oxygen. Cover the jar with breathable cloth + rubber band. Airtight lids cause off-flavours.

settings.question: It smells off — should I throw it out?
settings.answer: Healthy kefir smells tangy, yeasty, slightly cheesy. Bad smells are: vomit-like, rotten egg, or strong alcohol. If those, dump that batch and try once more with fresh milk and a thoroughly cleaned jar. If still bad after batch 2, contact us for a replacement.

settings.question: The kefir separated into curds and whey
settings.answer: You over-fermented (too long or too warm). Stir it back together; it's still safe to drink. Next batch, ferment 4–6 hours less or move the jar somewhere cooler.

settings.question: Fermentation is taking too long (>30 hours)
settings.answer: Two common causes: (1) your kitchen is too cold (under 20°C) — move grains to a warmer spot; (2) the grain-to-milk ratio is too low — use less milk per spoon of grains.

settings.question: I see fuzzy mold on the surface
settings.answer: Discard the batch and the grains. Mold (any color other than the natural cream/white of the grains themselves) means contamination. Contact us for a replacement starter — we ship within 2 working days.

settings.question: I'm going on vacation. What do I do with my grains?
settings.answer: Up to 14 days: cover grains in fresh milk and refrigerate. Up to 6 months: freeze grains in milk in an airtight container. To revive frozen grains, thaw in fridge for 24h, then run 2–3 normal batches at room temperature.

settings.quote: Tried wild grains from a friend before — totally unreliable. These are bulletproof. Same kefir every single batch for the past 5 months.

settings.question: How long do the grains live?
settings.answer: Indefinitely if cared for. Most home fermenters pass grains down for 5–10+ years.

settings.question: Will the grains multiply?
settings.answer: Yes — they double in mass every 2–4 weeks. By month 3 you'll have ~4× the starting amount. Share, freeze, or sell the surplus.

settings.question: Can I use plant milk?
settings.answer: Milk kefir grains specifically need lactose to thrive. Coconut milk works short-term (3–4 batches), then move grains back to dairy to refresh. For plant-based fermentation, our water-kefir grains are a better fit.

settings.question: How much grain do I need per litre of milk?
settings.answer: 1 tablespoon (~10g) of grains per 500ml–1L of milk. As your grains multiply, you can scale up batch size.

settings.question: Do you ship live grains via courier?
settings.answer: Yes — packed in milk inside an insulated, leak-proof pouch. Reaches you in 2–4 days across India. If grains arrive looking dormant or off, our replacement guarantee covers a free re-ship.
